steps.title: Move the first term and combine fractions
steps.content:
Subtract $\frac{1}{5xy}$ from both sides to isolate $xyz$:
$$xyz = \frac{1}{4yz} - \frac{1}{5xy}$$
Find a common denominator ($20xy^2z$) to combine the right side:
$$xyz = \frac{5x - 4z}{20xy^2z}$$

steps.title: Eliminate y from the denominator
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $y$:
$$xy^2z = \frac{5x - 4z}{20xz}$$
Divide both sides by $xz$ to isolate $y^2$:
$$y^2 = \frac{5x - 4z}{20x^2z^2}$$

steps.title: Take the square root to solve for y
steps.content: Extract $y$ by taking the square root of both sides: $$y = \sqrt{\frac{5x - 4z}{20x^2z^2}}$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
